summary: Matched Runtime/runtime.c by replacing the empty/nonmatching runtime source with inline PowerPC/MWCC assembly implementations for several compiler runtime helpers, updating runtime.h prototypes to ASM declarations, moving __div2i out of lbtime.h, and flipping Runtime/runtime.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev report confirms 100% matches for the added runtime functions and a +2644 byte matched-code gain.
what_changed: configure.py changed TRKLib's Runtime/runtime.c object from Object(NonMatching, "Runtime/runtime.c") to Object(Matching, "Runtime/runtime.c").
src/Runtime/runtime.c grew from an effectively empty file to 601 lines including <placeholder.h>, runtime.h, a static __constants table, and inline ASM definitions guarded by #ifdef __MWERKS__.
Added matched runtime helper bodies for __cvt_fp2unsigned, __div2u, __div2i, __mod2u, __mod2i, __shl2i, __shr2u, __shr2i, __cvt_sll_dbl, __cvt_sll_flt, and __cvt_dbl_usll.
Used #pragma push / #pragma optimization_level 0 / #pragma optimizewithasm off around some inline-asm routines, notably the signed/unsigned divide and modulo helpers.
src/Runtime/runtime.h replaced ad-hoc or typed prototypes with ASM void declarations for runtime helpers and a typed ASM declaration for __cvt_fp2unsigned(register double d).

runtime.h added declarations for __cvt_ull_dbl, __cvt_ull_flt, and __cvt_dbl_ull; the diff excerpt only shows implementations for other functions, so the reason for these declarations is not directly evidenced here.
src/melee/lb/lbtime.h removed the local extern s64 __div2i(s64, s64), consolidating the compiler runtime helper declaration under Runtime/runtime.h.
The automated decomp.dev comment reported matched code increasing to 25.99% (+0.07%, +2644 bytes) and linked code to 19.57% (+0.04%, +1632 bytes).
smart_moves: Kept compiler runtime helpers as ASM functions instead of attempting risky C rewrites, which is appropriate for low-level 64-bit arithmetic and floating-point conversion helpers where register behavior and condition codes matter.
Guarded inline assembly with #ifdef __MWERKS__ and provided a simple non-MWERKS fallback for __cvt_fp2unsigned, preserving host/tool compatibility where possible.
Moved __div2i out of lbtime.h, avoiding a local gameplay/library header owning a compiler runtime symbol.
Used runtime.h as the central declaration point for runtime helper symbols, reducing scattered extern declarations.
Only marked runtime.c as Matching after adding the corresponding matched source and receiving automated confirmation of 100% matches for the listed runtime functions.
Used explicit compiler pragmas to control optimization/asm interaction around functions whose matching is sensitive to MWCC inline-asm codegen.
decomp_lessons: Compiler support/runtime objects may be best matched with carefully transcribed inline assembly, especially for 64-bit division/modulo, shifts, and float/integer conversion routines.

When matching inline assembly in MWCC, function prototypes often need to be declared with ASM and void register-level signatures rather than high-level C argument lists, unless the function requires a typed C-visible interface like __cvt_fp2unsigned(register double d).
If a compiler runtime helper is referenced from gameplay code, declare it in the Runtime header rather than duplicating externs in unrelated subsystem headers.
Build metadata must be updated from NonMatching to Matching once the object is actually matched; configure.py is part of the matching change, not just source code.
Optimization pragmas such as #pragma optimization_level 0 and #pragma optimizewithasm off can be necessary matching tools for inline-assembly-heavy MWCC runtime functions.
Use small static constant tables when the original runtime routine loads exact floating-point constants by address; __cvt_fp2unsigned used a __constants table loaded with lis/ori and lfd.
naming_conventions: Compiler runtime helper symbols retain double-underscore names, e.g. __div2u, __div2i, __mod2u, __mod2i, __shl2i, __shr2u, __shr2i.
Conversion helper names encode source and destination types, e.g. __cvt_fp2unsigned, __cvt_sll_dbl, __cvt_sll_flt, __cvt_dbl_usll.

Internal inline-assembly labels use local/simple names such as lab1, lab2, positive1, no_adjust, func_end, or address-like labels such as L_802BA610 and lbl_802b2b1c when matching known assembly labels.
Runtime declarations belong in src/Runtime/runtime.h rather than subsystem headers like src/melee/lb/lbtime.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used ASM function declarations and inline PowerPC instructions wrapped in #ifdef __MWERKS__ blocks.
Used nofralloc in several assembly routines to control function prologue/epilogue generation.
Used explicit stack frame setup such as stwu r1,-16(r1), manual stores/loads, and final addi r1,r1,16 / blr where needed.
Used PowerPC condition registers and branches directly, including cr0/cr6/cr7 comparisons in __cvt_fp2unsigned and signedness handling in __div2i and __mod2i.
Used cntlzw, mtctr, bdnz, adde, subfc, and subfe patterns to implement 64-bit division and modulo at the register level.
Used subfic/subfze sign adjustment patterns for signed 64-bit division, modulo, and signed integer conversion behavior.
Used bitfield/rotate instructions such as rlwinm, rlwimi, rotlwi, rotrwi, clrlwi, extrwi, and extlwi in floating-point conversion helpers.
Applied #pragma push/pop with #pragma optimization_level 0 and #pragma optimizewithasm off to stabilize code generation around some inline-asm routines.
Verified matches through decomp.dev, which listed each added runtime function as 100.00% matched.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev report confirmed runtime.c improvements: __cvt_fp2unsigned, __div2u, __div2i, __mod2u, __mod2i, __shl2i, __shr2u, __shr2i, __cvt_sll_flt, and __cvt_dbl_usll reached 100.00%.
Automated report also showed collateral matching gains in several vi files, but the PR diff evidence does not explain the causal mechanism beyond runtime.c becoming matched/linked.
follow_up_queries: Check whether __cvt_ull_dbl, __cvt_ull_flt, and __cvt_dbl_ull are implemented elsewhere or were only added as forward declarations in runtime.h.
Search for remaining local extern declarations of compiler runtime helpers in non-Runtime headers and consolidate them into Runtime/runtime.h if appropriate.
Use PR1498 as a reference when matching other MWCC runtime/compiler helper objects that require nofralloc inline assembly and optimization pragmas.
Investigate why matching Runtime/runtime.c caused decomp.dev to report additional vi function match gains, if future dependency/linkage work needs an explanation.
